On August 1, 2022, Frank Kotter changed the car's oil himself.
On September 10, 2022, after heavy rainfall, Frank Kotter found the interior floor of the Passat flooded with about four inches of water and the car would not start.
Jane and Frank Kotter leased a Volkswagen Passat from 2018 to 2021 from Metro Volkswagen/Audi in New York City.
The Kotters had the car towed to Metro's service department in Manhattan; the tow cost $125.00 borne by them.

Josef Schmidt, service manager at Metro, informed the Kotters that the interior carpets were a total loss and the transmission control module (TCM) needed replacement due to water damage; total repair cost $3,790.00.
Jane and Frank Kotter live at 1350 East 85th Street, Brooklyn.
The Kotters paid the $3,790.00 repair bill and returned to Brooklyn.
On May 1, 2022, Frank Kotter purchased a new Volkswagen Passat for $30,500.00 with a standard three-year/36,000 mile warranty and an additional one-year transmission warranty for $500.00.
After research, the Kotters learned that other Passats had water entry problems due to a blocked plenum drain, and they decided to seek legal advice.
Volkswagen's customer service denied warranty coverage, stating the Kotters failed to clear debris below the windshield.
The defendant is Volkswagen and the plaintiffs are the Kotters.
